title: Lecture: Ecosystems

segments.text: An ecosystem is a community of living organisms interacting with their physical environment.
It includes plants, animals, microorganisms, and the soil, water, and air around them.
Energy enters the system through sunlight, which plants convert into chemical energy through photosynthesis.
This energy then moves up the food chain.
When organisms die, decomposers break them down, returning nutrients to the soil.

transcript: Today we'll discuss ecosystems. An ecosystem is a community of living organisms interacting with their physical environment. It includes plants, animals, microorganisms, and the soil, water, and air around them. Energy enters the system through sunlight, which plants convert into chemical energy through photosynthesis. This energy then moves up the food chain. When organisms die, decomposers break them down, returning nutrients to the soil. The cycle continues.
